prompt: Classify a snippet that initializes data = [] and writes data[key] = value.
expected_properties: labels it toaststunt
mentions [] is a map literal
mentions map indexing assignment
does not call [] a portable empty list

prompt: Classify a helper that calls $object_utils:isa(object, parent) rather than isa(object, parent).
expected_properties: labels the code portable-style when the target core has the utility
distinguishes core utility verb from server builtin
does not label it ToastStunt solely because ToastStunt has isa() builtin
uses long corified reference

prompt: Classify a snippet that calls xml_parse_tree(xml).
expected_properties: labels it patch-specific
states XML builtins came from a non-stock public patch
does not call it Sindome-specific
does not call it portable

prompt: Classify a snippet that uses ["enabled" -> true] and returns a BOOL value.
expected_properties: classifies as toaststunt
mentions MAP literal
mentions true BOOL value
does not label it portable
includes dialect_reason guidance

prompt: Classify a snippet that calls recreate(object, parent) as a builtin.
expected_properties: classifies as toaststunt
mentions recreate() builtin
distinguishes builtin from a core/package verb named recreate
does not label as LambdaMOO portable

prompt: Classify a snippet that directly calls $spatial:path_to(from, to).
expected_properties: classifies as core-specific in this corpus
mentions $spatial is a Sindome-specific utility package
does not call $spatial a MOO language feature
distinguishes direct package dependency from portable graph-search adaptation

prompt: Classify an example adapted from $spatial:path_to that uses only alist graphs, lists, while loops, recursion, and $list_utils:assoc.
expected_properties: can classify the adapted code as portable-style
mentions provenance may be approved Sindome-derived
does not claim the original $spatial package is portable
labels by the code actually shown

prompt: Classify a snippet that directly calls descendants(object).
expected_properties: classifies as toaststunt in this corpus
mentions descendants() is a ToastStunt builtin
contrasts with portable children() frontier traversal
does not label the builtin call portable

prompt: Classify a MOO implementation that walks children(object) with an explicit frontier to gather descendants.
expected_properties: classifies the implementation as portable-style
mentions it uses ordinary MOO loops and children()
does not call it ToastStunt merely because ToastStunt has descendants()
notes core behavior may still affect object tree semantics

prompt: Classify a snippet that calls $map_utils:dotkey on ToastStunt MAP values.
expected_properties: classifies as core-specific in this corpus
mentions $map_utils is a Sindome-specific package
mentions MAP values require ToastStunt/Stunt support
does not call the package portable

prompt: Classify a snippet that calls xml_parse_document(xml) in a MOO with a public XML patch installed.
expected_properties: classifies as patch-specific
mentions it is not stock LambdaMOO
mentions it is not baseline ToastStunt
does not call it Sindome-specific solely because Sindome-derived code may use it
